'''Joe Wardle''' (born 22 September 1991) is a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|sr}} forward and is currently assistant-coach for the [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL League 1]].

He previously played for the [[Bradford Bulls]], [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]<ref name=wardlejoinsgiants>{{cite news|title=Huddersfield Giants sign Bradford Bulls' Wardle|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/huddersfield/9044456.stm|work=BBC Sport|access-date=12 March 2014|date=29 September 2010}}</ref> and the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]], and the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]. He spent time on loan from the [[Huddersfield Giants|Giants]] at the [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] in 2011. He has also spent time on loan from [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] at Huddersfield in the Super League.

==Background==
Wardle was born in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire]], England.

==Career==
===Bradford Bulls===
Wardle came through the ranks at [[Bradford Bulls]] after signing from Illingworth A.R.L.F.C.<ref>{{cite web|last=Murphy|first=Connor|title=Bulls début next to Menzies 'amazing' for teenage centre|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/8202791.Chance_to_play_with_legend_leaves_Wardle_yearning_for_more|work=Bradford Telegraph and Argus|publisher=Newsquest (Yorkshire & North East) Ltd|accessdate=12 March 2014}}</ref>

Wardle made his début for the Bradford Bulls against [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Challenge Cup]] in May 2010.<ref>{{cite news|title=Bradford Bulls 22-26 Warrington Wolves|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8708224.stm|work=BBC Sport|access-date=12 March 2014|date=30 May 2010}}</ref> He débuted in the [[Super League]] a week later against the [[Huddersfield Giants]]. He made his first start for the club in the next game against [[Crusaders Rugby League|Crusaders RL]], but suffered an injury which prevented him from establishing a regular place in the first team. He returned to play for the under-20's team after recovering from the injury, but suffered a torn [[quadriceps]], which would keep him out of action for the rest of the season.<ref>{{cite web|last=Murphy|first=Connor|title=Bradford Bulls centre Wardle recalls day his season 'fell to bits'|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/8338945.Bradford_Bulls_centre_Wardle_on_day_his_season__fell_to_bits_|work=Bradford Telegraph and Argus|publisher=Newsquest (Yorkshire & North East) Ltd|accessdate=12 March 2014|date=18 August 2010}}</ref> In September 2010, he rejected a new contract from Bradford, and instead signed for the [[Huddersfield Giants]].<ref name=wardlejoinsgiants />

===Huddersfield Giants===
In 2011, he spent time on loan at the [[Barrow Raiders]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Barrow Raiders snap up Huddersfield Giants trio|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/9402087.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=29 July 2015|date=18 February 2011}}</ref> After performing well in the first half of the season, he was given a two-year contract extension, keeping him at the [[Galpharm Stadium]] until the end of 2014.<ref>{{cite web|title=Joe Wardle extends Huddersfield Giants contract|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14013367|work=BBC Sport|access-date=12 March 2014|date=4 July 2011}}</ref> In July 2013, he was rewarded with a further contract extension until 2017.<ref>{{cite web|title=Huddersfield Giants centre Joe Wardle extends deal|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23419442|work=BBC Sport|access-date=12 March 2014|date=23 July 2013}}</ref> He was named the [[2014 Huddersfield Giants season]]'s player of the year.

===Newcastle Knights===
During the 2016 season, Wardle signed a contract with [[National Rugby League]] side the [[Newcastle Knights]], starting in 2018.<ref>{{cite web|author=ROBERT DILLON30 Sep 2016, 5:27 p.m.|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/4200481/brown-stone-in-talks/?cs=306|title=Brown, Stone in talks | Newcastle Herald|publisher=Theherald.com.au|date=2016-09-30|access-date=2016-12-06}}</ref> However, he was able to gain a release in December to join the Newcastle Knights a year early ahead of the 2017 season, on a 3-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|author=NewcastleKnights.com.au|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/knights-sign-joe-wardle-24779|title=Knights sign Joe Wardle|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2014-04-21|accessdate=2016-12-20}}</ref>

He made his Newcastle Knights début in round 4 of the [[2017 NRL season|2017 season]] against the [[Penrith Panthers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/updated-team-lists-panthers-v-knights/tabid/10874/newsid/104951/default.aspx |title=Updated team lists: Panthers v Knights |publisher=NRL.com |date=2016-12-18 |accessdate=2017-03-24}}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers===
Wardle only played 17 matches for the Newcastle Knights after becoming homesick and gaining a release to return to England on a 3-year contract with Super League side the [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY5Ni00LS0tLS0tMTUzNzEtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List - Rugby League Project|first=Shawn Dollin and Andrew|last=Ferguson|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|accessdate=11 September 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=5602|title=Tigers swoop on Wardle|accessdate=11 September 2017}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions===
On 15 October 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=15 Oct 2021|accessdate=26 Dec 2021|title=Joe Wardle targets promotion after making Championship move|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/joe-wardle-targets-promotion-after-making-championship-move/}}</ref>
In round 2 of the 2023 Super League season, Wardle suffered an arm injury in the clubs 14-6 loss against the [[Catalans Dragons]]. Wardle was later ruled out for an indefinite period. In round 20 of the 2023 Super League season, Wardle made his return to the Leigh side as they were defeated by rivals [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] 44-18.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leigh-leopards-challenge-cup-hull-8631960/|title=Leigh Leopards suffer huge injury blow before Challenge Cup Final against Hull KR|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>
Wardle played 11 games for Leigh in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fifth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in their elimination playoff loss against Hull Kington Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://leighleopards.co.uk/leigh-leopards-2023-in-review/#:~:text=Overall%20playing%20record%3A,For%20694%2C%20Against%20580./|title=Leigh Leopards 2023 season in review|website=www.leighleopards.co.uk}}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC===
On 10 September 2023, it was reported that he had signed for [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL League 1]] on a two-year deal as player/assistant-coach.<ref>{{cite web|website=Roughyeds|date=10 Sep 2023|accessdate=28 Sep 2023|title=Now Joe Wardle signs as strengthening goes on|url=https://roughyeds.co.uk/2023/09/now-joe-wardle-signs-as-strengthening-goes-on/}}</ref>

==International career==
In October and November 2013, he played for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] in their [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] campaign.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}

In October and November 2014, he played in the [[2014 European Cup (rugby league)|2014 European Cup]] competition.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}
